behavior_summary: U8P_LIGTZAP::gotHit implements a one-shot lightning zap reaction when the object is struck by a valid overlapping source: it guards against re-entry, schedules and shows lightning visuals and SFX, animates a temporary sprite, applies a directional effect to the hit NPC, and then clears the guard so the zapper can trigger again.
The routine only runs when a non-zero hit source parameter is provided, the hit source overlaps this item (Item::overlaps), and a global guard alreadyHitLightningZapper is clear.

Visuals are produced by spawning a LightningBolt process, creating a temporary item sprite (Item::create), placing it near the object (Item::getX/Y/Z + offsets, Item::pop), playing a sound (playSFX), animating frames (Item::setFrame in a 0..4 loop with timed/spawned delays), and destroying the sprite (Item::destroy) after the animation/delay.

After the visual sequence the code obtains the NPC's facing direction (Npc::getDir) and calls METHOD::0080 with that direction and object pointers to apply the zap's gameplay effect (likely damage/knockback), and finally clears alreadyHitLightningZapper to allow future triggers.
notable_control_flow: Early exit if the hit-source parameter (BP+0Ah) is zero.
Guard check of global alreadyHitLightningZapper prevents starting the sequence if already set.
Item::overlaps(hitSource, this) is used as a gating collision test; the sequence proceeds only if overlap returns true.
Sequence uses urandom + spawn to introduce randomized/staggered delays for visuals and uses free/timed helper processes to step through animation frames and schedule destruction.
The global guard is set at sequence start and cleared only after the full visual/effect sequence completes.
Loop/frame progression inside U8P_LIGTZAP::gotHit; treated as control-flow, not explicit internal call.
open_questions: Exact semantics of METHOD::0080: inferred to apply the actual lightning effect (damage/knockback) but purpose is not explicit.
Precise meaning of the temporary item type/id created (0x01FF or similar) and how its frames map to the lightning animation.
Whether LightningBolt process does additional gameplay effects separate from METHOD::0080, or is purely visual.
Timing details and exact spawn helper implementations (FREE::1C19 / FREE::29D8) are inferred from behaviour but not fully specified.

main_function.name: U8P_LIGTZAP::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Respond to an incoming hit by spawning lightning visuals and SFX, apply a directional gameplay effect to the target NPC, and prevent immediate retriggers using a global guard.
entry_conditions: Parameter BP+0Ah (hit source) must be non-zero.
Item::overlaps(BP+0Ah, BP+06h) must return true (hit source overlaps this object).

function_map.name: U8P_LIGTZAP::gotHit
function_map.role: Event handler for hit events; orchestrates guard, visuals, SFX, effect application, and cleanup.

function_map.name: Item::overlaps
function_map.role: Collision test used as gating check before triggering the zap sequence.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Provide random delay/variation used to stagger spawned helper timing.

function_map.name: spawn
function_map.role: Create timed/free helper processes used for scheduling frame advances and delayed destroy.

function_map.name: U8P_LIGTZAP::process LightningBolt
function_map.role: Start/run lightning visual process; may also encapsulate some effect logic.

function_map.name: Item::create
function_map.role: Create temporary sprite/item used for the lightning visual.

function_map.name: Item::getX
function_map.role: Read created item's X coordinate for positioning the pop location.

function_map.name: Item::getY
function_map.role: Read created item's Y coordinate for positioning the pop location.

function_map.name: Item::getZ
function_map.role: Read created item's Z coordinate for positioning the pop location.

function_map.name: Item::pop
function_map.role: Place/activate the temporary sprite at an offset position to display the visual.

function_map.name: U8P_LIGTZAP::playSFX
function_map.role: Play the lightning sound effect when the zap triggers.

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: Animate the temporary sprite through frames (0..4) during the visual sequence.

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: Destroy the temporary sprite after animation/delay.

function_map.name: Npc::getDir
function_map.role: Obtain NPC facing to pass into METHOD::0080 so the effect can be applied directionally.

function_map.name: METHOD::0080
function_map.role: Apply the zap's gameplay effect (likely damage/knockback) using direction and object pointers; exact semantics inferred.

external_calls.purpose: Confirm the hit source actually overlaps this object before triggering the zap sequence.
Generate small random values to vary spawn/timing of visual/effect helper processes.
Create timed/free helper processes to delay steps, animate frames, and schedule destruction.
Start a LightningBolt visual/process at trigger time (may be purely visual or partially gameplay-related).
Create a temporary sprite/item used to display the lightning visual.
Read created item's X coordinate to compute pop position (offset by -50).
Read created item's Y coordinate to compute pop position (offset by -50).
Read Z coordinate to keep vertical positioning consistent for the pop.
Place the temporary sprite into the world at a computed offset to display the lightning.
Play the zap SFX (SFX id 0x00C8) when the effect triggers.
Advance the temporary sprite through animation frames (0..4) during the effect.
Remove the temporary visual sprite after the animation completes or after a delay.
Obtain the target NPC's facing direction to parameterize the gameplay effect.
Apply the zap's gameplay consequence (inferred: damage, push, or other effect) using direction and object pointers.

reads.name: alreadyHitLightningZapper
reads.likely_effect: Read as a guard: must be 0 to allow the zap sequence to run and avoid re-entry.

writes.name: alreadyHitLightningZapper
writes.likely_effect: Written to 1 when the zap sequence starts to prevent retriggers, and cleared back to 0 after the sequence completes so the zapper can be used again.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_LIGTZAP::gotHit implements a one-shot lightning zap reaction when the object is struck by a valid overlapping source: it guards against re-entry, schedules and shows lightning visuals and SFX, animates a temporary sprite, applies a directional effect to the hit NPC, and then clears the guard so the zapper can trigger again.
